title: Whose really in charge of Wille
display_title: Whose really in charge of Wille
wikitext:
[]: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Dec 30, 2016 4:13 am In the "Shinji Hate" thread there was this remark about Misato: BlueBasilisk wrote: She could be in serious shit with her superiors in Wille

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 30, 2016 5:46 am I see no reason to assume that any superiors exist; None a ever mentioned and a big deal is made of the Wunder being humanity's last hope. I mean in a chaotic environment panic can't be risked so there might be some pressure to mantain her crew's morale and loyalty but so far as things go, her underlings seem to like & admire her. They had Sakura's VA do her lines exty times so she sounds respectful enough. I imagine that if your name is not Shinji, Misato is still to an extent the bold, optimistic & emphatic leader she used to be, through more pragmatic to fit the terrifying world of Q. (As Asuka notes.) See also how Gendo notes that "Katsuragi's" actions are within parameters suggesting that she's WILLE's mastermind. That said, they might have recruited from other sources - See that guy who might have been a former co-worker of Kaji's -

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 30, 2016 5:58 am I'd just assume that Wille is decentralized and doesn't have a leader per se. There's likely much more to the organization than what we see, so Misato might be in charge of their principal/only military force, while having equals who precide over other operations.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Fri Dec 30, 2016 6:01 am In my Post 3.33 Fic, I kinda went with the idea that WILLE has a group up at the top ahead of Misato who manage things like the remaining human settlements and so on but because of who Misato is, her history and relationship with NERV she and the Wunder are pretty much given free reign to do their own thing. That's purely just fanwanking though.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Dec 30, 2016 6:06 am Watching the movie I got the feeling she's the one in charge. Honestly I felt that she's WILLE, she takes everything on her shoulders and just goes on. I bet she founded WILLE after NTI both to save humanity and with guilty to what she said to Shinji. She had total control on what to do with Eva 01 and she was the one that had to decide what to do with Shinji, so I think she doesn't really have no one over her head.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Dec 30, 2016 6:55 am My impression after watching the movie is that Wille is either a UN organization (the blue armbands, the ships, the amount of resources at their disposal) or a multi-national coalition made up of former UN members that's basically a lateral move from Nerv. Misato's role on Wunder seems equivalent to Gendo's role as supreme commander, and even he had people to answer to (not that he ever did other than Seele...). I think there'll be someone higher up the totem pole than Misato, whether that's Kaji, a UN council or some other body we haven't seen before.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Dec 30, 2016 6:58 am pwhodges wrote: But it seems to me that it is usually assumed she's in charge (whether alone or jointly with Rits and maybe Kaji). At no point in the story is there any indication that there is anyone who was previously above her in the hierarchy other than Gendou (+ Fuyu) and Seele. I suppose one could conjecture that Wille was set up by the Japanese government, but there's no hint of that shown. What do other people think?

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 30, 2016 8:17 am Wille seemed to me like a make-shift idealist crew built out of whatever pieces-parts they could scrounge up. (Misato feels like she needs an S2 Engine in the Wunder, so she goes out and gets an S2 Engine for the Wunder. No formal signatures and approval required.) Having "Superiors" above Misato would suggest that Wille has an annual budget or something that Misato needs to adhere to. There's no way an organization with a budget and red tape would hire on Midori Kitakami, who sounded like she was only starting to figure out how all this junk worked and what her full responsibilities were. "Superiors" worth any salt would not approve the hiring of such a person. Much of the crew sounds like they were interview by Misato (or Kaji) simply asking "You wanna make a better tomorrow?" or "You hate Nerv?" (Engineers were probably interviewed by Ritsuko and maybe even Maya. Misato's interaction would them would probably be "You know how to work this thing?" and "You hate Nerv?") Also, I don't think anyone in Wille is getting paid anything. I see them as a bunch of freedom fighters desperately banding together to keep Nerv from destroying everything. Everyone at Wille seems to be working for a cause and are rewarded with food and shelter. There is no apparent budget beyond "What can we steal today?"

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Dec 30, 2016 9:27 am jcmoorehead wrote: In my Post 3.33 Fic, I kinda went with the idea that WILLE has a group up at the top ahead of Misato who manage things like the remaining human settlements and so on but because of who Misato is, her history and relationship with NERV she and the Wunder are pretty much given free reign to do their own thing. That's purely just fanwanking though.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Geometer » Fri Dec 30, 2016 1:04 pm I would assume that she may not have superiors but still "answers to" whats left of the UN or whatever attempt at governance is left.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Dec 30, 2016 2:28 pm I'm surprised no one has said the one obvious answer to this question: Gendo. He's always been the one pulling all the strings. Just like he manipulated Shinji, he manipulated his very own staff at Nerv into thinking they rebelled against him by forming Wille, when in reality they're working to serve his ends. Everything they do happens within "acceptable parameters".

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 30, 2016 3:24 pm ^ Certainly, but I don't think Misato answers to Gendo. Gendo is just really good at manipulating people into doing what he wants.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Dec 30, 2016 4:45 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ^ Certainly, but I don't think Misato answers to Gendo. Gendo is just really good at manipulating people into doing what he wants.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Geometer » Fri Dec 30, 2016 5:53 pm Bagheera wrote: Or rather, he's good at spewing bullshit and claiming that anything that happens, no matter the particulars, plays into his hands. If he was as good as he says he is 3I would have gone off without a hitch and we'd be done with this already.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 30, 2016 5:55 pm Bagheera wrote: Or rather, he's good at spewing bullshit and claiming that anything that happens, no matter the particulars, plays into his hands. If he was as good as he says he is 3I would have gone off without a hitch and we'd be done with this already.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Dec 30, 2016 6:05 pm Geometer wrote: Well why would he just life to himself and Futuyski? Not like hes has to impress anyone, except the audience.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Lennik » Sat Dec 31, 2016 12:26 am Gendo is definitely bluffing, or maybe he's just deluded. At several points in 1.0 and 2.0 he talks about how his plan has almost come to fruition, and yet 14 years later he's still having trouble. He's delusional. That's why he thinks Yui is going to accept him with open arms and why he has no idea why the dummy plug refuses to work at the climax of 2.0 and Shinji has to come and bail him out. The fact that Yui refuses to operate without Shinji because she loves her son appears to be totally lost on him, and that simple human emotion nearly threw a wrench in his plans that would have gotten all of humanity killed, him included, if Shinji hadn't decided to return and fight Zeruel. Gendo is flying by the seat of his pants. If his goal is still instrumentality, I predict what screws it up for him will be his own son doing something as simple as making a decision, and Gendo will never see it coming, because how can someone descended from him not be a nihilistic loser who wants to live in the past?

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sat Dec 31, 2016 8:56 am ^Seele did the same thing. Kaworu said the promised time had arrived when Mark.06 descended to earth, but nothing came of that until 14 years later either. Seems like they both had to regroup after whatever happened during 3rd Impact. I think Gendo's strength isn't that he has everything plotted out, but that he's able to adapt to an ever-changing situation and twist things to his advantage. Doesn't always work out as he found out with Unit 01 and the dummy plug. Even his victory over Seele was a "this will do for now." Gendo hasn't said what he's plotting yet, but based on some of the things he and Fuyutsuki have said, it appears that Yui was involved in masterminding it and her entering Unit 01 was all part of the plan. Geometer wrote: I would assume that she may not have superiors but still "answers to" whats left of the UN or whatever attempt at governance is left.

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 31, 2016 12:55 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Wille seemed to me like a make-shift idealist crew built out of whatever pieces-parts they could scrounge up. (Misato feels like she needs an S2 Engine in the Wunder, so she goes out and gets an S2 Engine for the Wunder. No formal signatures and approval required.) Having "Superiors" above Misato would suggest that Wille has an annual budget or something that Misato needs to adhere to. There's no way an organization with a budget and red tape would hire on Midori Kitakami, who sounded like she was only starting to figure out how all this junk worked and what her full responsibilities were. "Superiors" worth any salt would not approve the hiring of such a person. Much of the crew sounds like they were interview by Misato (or Kaji) simply asking "You wanna make a better tomorrow?" or "You hate Nerv?" (Engineers were probably interviewed by Ritsuko and maybe even Maya. Misato's interaction would them would probably be "You know how to work this thing?" and "You hate Nerv?") Also, I don't think anyone in Wille is getting paid anything. I see them as a bunch of freedom fighters desperately banding together to keep Nerv from destroying everything. Everyone at Wille seems to be working for a cause and are rewarded with food and shelter. There is no apparent budget beyond "What can we steal today?"

[]: Re: Who'se really in charge of Wille             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 31, 2016 8:06 pm Kendrix wrote: THIS. I very much got a 'ragtag bunch of survivors' vibe there, like, they are taking what they can get, manpower shortages are mentioned etc. I don't think there IS government anymore, aside from enclaves of survivors being run as citystates.
